products.old_post_content:
COSRX Salicylic Acid Daily Gentle Cleanser is a BHA-focused face wash for oily and acne-prone routines. Because the current ingredient data is thin, this sample only uses claims supported by the product name and category: salicylic acid cleansing, pore care, and daily face-wash use.

The cleanser makes most sense when your skin feels greasy, congested, or heavy after sunscreen. Pair it with a simple moisturiser after rinsing, and avoid layering too many exfoliating products on the same day if your skin starts to sting.

Key Benefits

  • BHA cleanser focus — Salicylic acid in the product name signals pore-care support for oily routines
  • Daily wash format — The rinse-off step helps refresh skin without leaving an active serum on all day
  • Congestion support — A cleanser like this suits areas that feel oily around the nose, chin, and forehead
  • Routine control — It lets acne-prone shoppers keep the cleansing step targeted while moisturiser stays simple

Key Ingredients

Salicylic Acid — A BHA commonly used in oily and blemish-prone skincare because it supports clearer-feeling pores.

Cleanser Base — Full INCI was not available in Emart's structured data, so the sample avoids naming unsupported surfactants or concentrations.

Product Format — As a rinse-off cleanser, it should be treated differently from a leave-on BHA toner or serum.

Best For

  • Oily skin — targeted cleansing for sebum-heavy routines
  • Acne-prone skin — BHA-focused wash step
  • Combination skin — useful mainly on congested zones
  • Teen or beginner routines — simpler than starting with multiple leave-on actives

Targets These Concerns

  • Oily T-zone
  • Mild pore congestion
  • Breakout-prone skin
  • Heavy sunscreen feel after a long day

Not Recommended For

  • Salicylate sensitivity — avoid or patch test carefully.
  • Over-exfoliated skin — pause until stinging and flaking settle.
  • Very dry skin — use less often or choose a gentler hydrating cleanser.

How to Use

  1. Use on wet skin, avoiding the eye area.
  2. Massage briefly over oily or congested areas.
  3. Rinse fully with lukewarm water.
  4. Follow with a non-heavy moisturiser.

Routine Fit

Use this cleanser on days when your skin feels oily or congested. If your routine already includes AHA, BHA, retinol, or benzoyl peroxide, keep this wash gentle and monitor dryness.

Before Use: Patch test first if you are sensitive to salicylic acid or acne treatments. Stop use if irritation continues.

Disclaimer

Check the product carefully when receiving your order. After delivery is accepted, returns or exchanges depend on Emart's active return policy and product condition. Packaging can vary between batches, so the tube, box, or label may look slightly different from photos while the product remains the same original item.

products.old_post_content:
COSRX Advanced Snail Mucin Gel Cleanser is a gel cleanser for skin that wants a softer cleansing feel than a strong acne wash. The product name points to snail mucin as the hero ingredient, so this sample focuses on comfort, barrier feel, and gentle daily cleansing without inventing a full INCI list.

It suits routines where the cleanser should not be the most aggressive step. After rinsing, follow with a hydrating toner or moisturiser while the skin is still slightly damp, especially if air-conditioning or sun exposure makes your face feel tight.

Key Benefits

  • Cushiony cleanse — The snail mucin positioning suggests a softer, comfort-first wash experience
  • Barrier routine fit — This cleanser suits shoppers who want cleansing without a harsh squeaky finish
  • Hydration-friendly feel — The gel format works well before toner, essence, or moisturiser
  • Gentle daily role — It keeps the cleansing step calm when leave-on products do the heavier work

Key Ingredients

Snail Mucin — A well-known COSRX ingredient family associated with a soft, cushiony skin feel and barrier-support routines.

Gel Cleanser Base — Full INCI was not available in Emart's structured data, so this sample avoids unsupported surfactant claims.

Rinse-Off Format — Best understood as a gentle cleansing step, not a replacement for a leave-on snail essence.

Best For

  • Sensitive-feeling skin — calmer cleanse when strong foams feel too sharp
  • Combination skin — light gel texture without a heavy film
  • Dryness-prone routines — better when followed quickly with moisturiser
  • Snail mucin fans — cleanser option within the COSRX snail family

Targets These Concerns

  • Tight feeling after cleansing
  • Daily sweat and surface grime
  • Skin that prefers gentle Korean gel cleansers
  • Barrier-focused routines

Not Recommended For

  • Snail mucin sensitivity — avoid if snail products usually cause bumps or itching.
  • Heavy waterproof makeup — remove makeup first with oil or balm.
  • Very oily skin wanting a strong matte finish — choose a more clarifying cleanser instead.

How to Use

  1. Wet your face and hands with lukewarm water.
  2. Massage a small amount across the face for 20-30 seconds.
  3. Rinse well and avoid rubbing with a towel.
  4. Apply toner or moisturiser before skin fully dries.

Routine Fit

Use it as a morning cleanser or as a second cleanse after makeup and sunscreen are removed. It pairs best with simple hydrating steps rather than another strong cleansing product.

Before Use: Patch test first if you have reacted to snail mucin before. Stop use if irritation continues.

Disclaimer

Check the product carefully when receiving your order. After delivery is accepted, returns or exchanges depend on Emart's active return policy and product condition. Packaging can vary between batches, so the tube, box, or label may look slightly different from photos while the product remains the same original item.
